danger_signals.visual_form: Low-visibility maze or tunnel setting within game stage
danger_signals.meaning: Increased difficulty/challenge state; requires heightened player dexterity and reaction time

danger_signals.visual_form: Hurdles and environmental hazards appearing in sequence
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating challenge; danger of failing timing-based athletic events

safety_signals.visual_form: Open, well-lit Olympic-style stadium or training ground imagery
safety_signals.meaning: Safe, controlled competition environment; structured challenge space

safety_signals.visual_form: Game collects player Name, Age, Gender, Date for customized advice
safety_signals.meaning: System is adapting to player capability; game world acknowledges and accommodates player state

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Olympic rings (implicit in Athletic World challenge design)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stadium and track venues; official Olympic-style event zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Five distinct Olympic-style challenge arenas (Hurdles, Hop A Log, Animal Trail, Rafting, Dark Tunnel)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each challenge tests different physical skill: timing, coordination, dexterity, speed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark Tunnel stage represents peak difficulty; obstacle density and speed increase across stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Faster pad-step requirements, tighter timing windows, more complex movement sequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game displays customized advice based on player demographics and performance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty curve adjusts; feedback tailored to age/gender/performance profile

environmental_storytelling.example: Five distinct challenge types (Hurdles, Hop A Log, Animal Trail, Rafting, Dark Tunnel) convey an Olympic training complex narrative—each zone teaches specific athletic skill
Transition from clear outdoor venues (Hurdles, Rafting) to claustrophobic Dark Tunnel signals increasing challenge intensity and player skill progression
Physical Power Pad stepping is the world's primary interaction language—no story text needed; stepping on floor mat IS the athletic action

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Slower step response required; more forgiving timing windows in early challenges like Hop A Log
Faces Dark Tunnel challenge requiring rapid dexterity; tighter timing feedback from pad sensors
Likely incorporates unpredictable or natural hazards; tests adaptability over pure speed
Likely incorporates balance or rhythm elements; tests coordination and timing against water/flow dynamics

notes: Super Team Games is a physical sports-training game whose world-state is communicated entirely through (1) spatial/environmental design of five distinct Olympic-style challenge zones, (2) escalating difficulty via visual and mechanical progression, and (3) player-adaptive feedback. There are no factions, no political conflict, and no danger/safety signaling in a narrative sense—the 'danger' is purely performance-based challenge. The game's manifestation layer is minimal: world-state == player progression through athletic challenge tiers, signaled by arena type and pad sensor sensitivity. No faction colors, sigils, or enemy variants exist in documented sources.
